Analysis Period—
Market Regime Analysis
Market Regime Analysis — Up / Down CaptureBull & Bear Regimes · 10%+ Decline Threshold
Bull Regime
Bear Regime

A bear regime is triggered when the benchmark declines 10%+ from its prior peak. Up Capture = model ÷ benchmark during bulls. Down Capture = model ÷ benchmark during bears.

Trailing 1-Year Excess Risk / Return to Benchmark
Trailing 1-Year Excess Risk / Return to Benchmark (Monthly)Each Dot = 1 Month-End

X-axis = trailing 1-year std dev of model minus benchmark (right of center = more volatile than benchmark). Y-axis = trailing 1-year return of model minus benchmark (above center = outperformance). Upper-left quadrant = better return and less risk than benchmark. Orange diamond = most recent month-end (Mar 31, 2026).

Rolling 12-Month Return · Model vs. Benchmark + Difference
Rolling 1-Year Annualized Return with BenchmarkTrailing 12-Month · Lower Panel = Excess Return

Solid lines = model. Dashed lines = benchmark. Lower panel shows rolling excess return (model minus benchmark). Computed from the common start date.

Rolling 12-Month Standard Deviation · Model vs. Benchmark + Difference
Rolling 1-Year Annualized Volatility with BenchmarkTrailing 12-Month · Lower Panel = Δ Std Dev

Solid lines = model. Dashed lines = benchmark. Lower panel shows the difference in rolling standard deviation (model minus benchmark). Positive values = model more volatile than benchmark.

Disclosures

Index Creation — Hypothetical Backtesting and Index Design Comparison

In order to evaluate the efficacy of the Model, we conducted a comprehensive backtesting procedure which involved the creation of an index adhering to the design of the Model. The index construction was informed by historical data and a systematic approach. Executed at the close of each trading day following modifications to the index methodology, the constructed index comprised a portfolio of indexes representative of the asset classes specified within the Model's guidelines.

It is crucial to note that the index is exclusively designed for research purposes and is not intended for investment. Historical data was utilized to juxtapose the performance of our created index against a diversified benchmark consisting of global equity and/or fixed income indexes, spanning the chosen backtesting duration. The primary objective of this research was to evaluate the hypothetical performance of the proposed Model across varying market conditions, aiming to glean insights into its potential viability as an investment strategy.

General Disclosures

Modelist Inc. has reasonable belief that the material taken as a whole will not create any untrue or misleading implication regarding its services, investments, or client experiences. Each investor's situation is unique so please work with a professional financial advisor, tax accountant or legal representative, as applicable, to develop an individualized plan or address any questions you may have. This report does not replace the custodian statements provided directly to you from the custodian.

All investments come with a risk of loss. We do not provide tax, legal or accounting advice. Please consult with your financial, legal, and tax advisors regarding individual circumstances.

Please refer to advisorinfo.sec.gov for the advisor's ADV Part 2A, CRD No. 327365, for material risks and conflicts of interest disclosures.

Past performance does not indicate future performance. Modelist does not guarantee performance outcomes or cost reductions. Any strategies discussed are illustrative in nature and may not be suitable for all investors or advisors. The investment return and principal value of an investment will fluctuate; an investor's shares, when redeemed, may be worth more or less than their original cost; current performance may be lower or higher than the performance data quoted.

Important Disclosures

This report does not reflect the performance of any account actually managed by Modelist. The data contained in or used in generating this report has not been audited or verified by Modelist or any other party, and any use of this report should be made with this understanding. This report is not an official account statement or other official document of Modelist or any other party.

This report does not constitute financial, accounting, legal or tax advice. Please consult with your financial, legal and tax advisors for such advice.

These results do not reflect actual trading activity, portfolio management and investment decisions, or the impact of market conditions on investor behavior.

Criteria and Assumptions Used in Index Performance

All indexes represent hypothetical blended investments of weighted securities or indexes as designated by the creator of this report based on the expected financial situation of the intended audience and should be used for illustrative purposes only and should not be considered performance reports. They are calculated by taking a weighted average of the target weights and the securities or indices total return, assuming all dividends reinvested, since the latest rebalance date. These models are assumed to rebalance to the exact designated weights at each calendar day, week, month end, quarter end, or year end — whichever is chosen when setting up the index. No advisory fees, transaction costs or taxes are included. Index holdings are weighted by percentage, not whole share numbers.

The performance of an index is calculated by taking a weighted average of the stated target weights and the securities' and/or indexes' total return, assuming reinvestment of all dividends and other distributions on the related ex-date, since the latest rebalance date. The index(es) portrayed in this report are assumed to rebalance to the exact designated weights on a daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly or annual basis, whichever you and/or Modelist selected in generating this report. The performance illustrated in this report may assume that rebalancing occurred in a manner different from how an Advisor rebalances a client portfolio. An Advisor may recommend rebalancing when an asset class varies from its targeted allocation. The way an Advisor invests dividends may be different than how the index(es) invest dividends.

All stated target weights are based on allocation choices input by you and/or Modelist. These weights represent the values used at rebalance periods. All weightings ignore the concept of whole shares and instead use the exact percentage chosen when creating the index(es).

Unless otherwise noted, no transaction costs (e.g., commissions, sales loads), taxes, or advisory fees are deducted from the performance results generated by the index(es). Any expense ratio shown is inclusive of the underlying fees in the securities included in the index(es) (as reported by Morningstar Inc.), and as such should be considered for illustrative purposes only. As discussed above, such fees do not include transaction costs (e.g., commissions, sales loads), taxes, or advisory fees.

The stated yield for a hypothetical index is based on the weighted average of trailing 12-month yields for the underlying securities and/or indexes. It is no indication or guarantee of future yield.

Risks and Limitations of Hypothetical Performance

ACCORDINGLY, ALL INDEX RETURNS ARE HYPOTHETICAL OR SIMULATED AND NOT ACTUAL PERFORMANCE RESULTS AND SHOULD NOT BE CONSIDERED PERFORMANCE REPORTING. No representation is made that your investments will achieve results similar to those shown, and actual performance results may differ materially from those shown. Returns portrayed in this report do not reflect actual trading, portfolio management, and investment decisions, but are hypothetical or simulated results of a hypothetical portfolio over the time period indicated and do not reflect the performance of actual accounts managed by Modelist or any other person. The securities, indexes, and other components of the hypothetical index(es) were selected with the full benefit of hindsight, after their performance during the time period was known. In general, hypothetical returns generally exceed the results of client portfolios actually managed by advisors due to several factors, including the fact that actual portfolio allocations differed from the allocations represented by the market indexes used to create the hypothetical portfolios over the time periods shown, new research was applied at different times to the relevant indexes, and index performance does not reflect the deduction of any fees and expenses. Results also assume that the index design would not have changed over time and in response to market conditions, which is likely to have occurred if an actual account had been managed during the time period shown.

Hypothetical performance is presented for illustrative purposes only and is not intended to reflect the performance of any actual investment strategy offered to clients.

INVESTMENTS IN SECURITIES INVOLVE INVESTMENT RISKS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE LOSS OF PRINCIPAL AND FLUCTUATION IN VALUE. The investment return and principal value of securities and other financial instruments will fluctuate so that an investor's investments, when sold or redeemed, may be worth more or less than the original cost. Investment results are not guaranteed. No investment strategy (including asset allocation and diversification strategies) can guarantee a profit or protect against loss of principal.

THIS REPORT IS NOT AN INVESTMENT PERFORMANCE REPORT. DO NOT RELY ON THIS REPORT AS PORTRAYING, OR CONTAINING PERFORMANCE OF, AN ACTUAL ACCOUNT. THIS REPORT SHOWS HYPOTHETICAL OR SIMULATED RETURNS OF INDEX(ES) AND IS FOR ILLUSTRATIVE PURPOSES ONLY. The SEC has not approved or reviewed the returns being displayed within the report. This report is not intended to and does not predict or show the actual investment performance of any account. An index represents an investment in a hypothetical weighted blend of securities and/or indexes which, together with other inputs, were selected by you and/or Modelist and, accordingly, an index should be used for illustrative purposes only and does not reflect actual client trading, asset allocation decisions, or portfolio management.

PAST PERFORMANCE IS NOT INDICATIVE OF FUTURE RESULTS. To the extent this report portrays historical performance of particular securities, indexes or other financial instruments, past performance of such securities, indexes or other instruments is not indicative of future results. Different accounts (even if managed pursuant to the same strategy) can have varying results due to: 1) the period of time in which the accounts are active; 2) the timing of contributions and withdrawals; 3) the account size; 4) the minimum investment requirements and/or withdrawal restrictions; 5) the rate of advisory, brokerage commissions and transaction fees; and 6) restrictions or limitations on whether the account can be rebalanced.

ALL RISK CALCULATIONS ARE FOR ILLUSTRATIVE PURPOSES ONLY. They are calculated at the index level using a benchmark selected by you and/or your Advisor. The benchmark is displayed for comparison purposes and is used to calculate index level risk data when necessary.

Investment Risks

International/Emerging Market Equities: Investing in international securities involves special additional risks including currency risk, political risk, and risk associated with varying accounting standards. Investing in emerging markets may accentuate these risks.

Sector Strategies: Portfolios that invest exclusively in one sector or industry involve additional risks. The lack of industry diversification subjects the investor to increased industry-specific risks.

Small Cap Equities: Portfolios that invest in stocks of small companies involve additional risks. Smaller companies typically have a higher risk of failure and are not as well-established as larger blue-chip companies.

High-Yield Bonds: Portfolios that invest in lower-rated debt securities (junk bonds) involve additional risks because of the lower credit quality. The investor should be aware of the possible higher level of volatility, and increased risk of default.

Bonds: Bonds are subject to interest rate risk. As the prevailing level of bond interest rates rise, the value of bonds already held in a portfolio declines.

Bank Loan/Senior Debt: Bank loans are impacted by interest rate risk and default risk. They are often non-investment grade and relatively illiquid.

Liquidity Risk: ETF trading may be halted due to market conditions, impacting an investor's ability to sell a fund.

Market Price Risk: The market price of ETFs and closed-end funds traded on the secondary market is subject to the forces of supply and demand and thus independent of the NAV.

Money Market Funds: Investments in these funds are not guaranteed by the FDIC or any other government agency. You can lose money by investing in these funds.

Definitions

Alpha: A measure of an investment's performance compared to a benchmark, indicating the risk-adjusted excess return of the investment relative to the return of the benchmark.

Annualized Return: The geometric average amount of money earned by an investment each year over a given time period, expressed as a percentage.

Beta: A measure of the volatility, or systematic risk, of an investment compared to the benchmark.

Information Ratio: A risk-adjusted performance measure defined as the excess return of an investment divided by the tracking error.

Maximum Drawdown: The maximum observed loss from a peak to a trough of an investment, before a new peak is attained, measured as a percentage.

Rolling Period Over/Under Performance: The annualized total excess return (if positive) or underperformance (if negative) of an investment compared to its benchmark, evaluated on a monthly basis over the indicated look-back period.

Sharpe Ratio: A risk-adjusted measure calculated by dividing the excess return of an investment by its standard deviation, indicating the average return earned in excess of the risk-free rate per unit of volatility.

Standard Deviation: A measure of the dispersion of a set of data from its mean, calculated as the square root of variance, indicating the historical volatility of an investment.

Total Return: The actual rate of return of an investment over a given period, including both capital appreciation and any dividends or interest payments.

Tracking Error: A measure of how closely an investment follows its benchmark, calculated as the standard deviation of the difference between the investment and benchmark returns.
